action_metadata.metadata_title: Withdraw 1,193,000 ada for Intersect Technical Steering Committee Support

onchain_context:
onchain recipient history: stake address stake1784sdxt6jjennmstphgdu7l7c2scf5d02a6cve2dgn5s2kq5u3j9v appears in 14 withdrawal proposals totalling 57273260000000 lovelace (first 2026-06-23 16:14:33, last 2026-06-26 13:51:52)
onchain DRep vote counts so far: yes=45 no=12 abstain=5 (explicit ballots, not stake-weighted)
treasury flow (36 epochs): inflow 158838297854652 lovelace, enacted withdrawals 361435336000000 lovelace, ratio 2.275492

fact_verdicts.fact: 1. Action id is b3d452bff7769d7f557ec6b8974760ee6c5e496c276652b654032966621e0ccf#3; action type is TreasuryWithdrawals; title is "Withdraw 1,193,000 ada for Intersect Technical Steering Committee Support"; proposed epoch is 638 and expires after epoch 645.
fact_verdicts.note: Matches Source Material 0 exactly.

fact_verdicts.fact: 2. The on-chain withdrawal reward entry pays stake address stake1784sdxt6jjennmstphgdu7l7c2scf5d02a6cve2dgn5s2kq5u3j9v with value "1193000000000" lovelace.
fact_verdicts.note: The anchor onChain gov_action rewards entry has that key and value.

fact_verdicts.fact: 3. The anchor states the TSC requests "$298,250 (1,193,000 ADA)" to support activities for 12 months.
fact_verdicts.note: Stated in the anchor motivation.

fact_verdicts.fact: 4. The anchor states the proposal is submitted by Intersect on behalf of the vendor and that Abstract, Motivation, and Rationale were sourced from the approved vendor proposal submitted as part of the Intersect budget process.
fact_verdicts.note: Stated in the anchor abstract.

fact_verdicts.fact: 5. The anchor describes three work packages: WP 1 funds attendance of technical experts at major ecosystem events and commissioning of technical reports; WP 2 funds the Parameter Committee, CIP editors, and Hard Fork Working Group; WP 3 funds establishment of a pilot independent technical review programme launching in 2027.
fact_verdicts.note: Stated in the anchor motivation.

fact_verdicts.fact: 6. The anchor says three TSC-related activity areas are funded separately through the main Intersect budget and are not included here: technical workshops, the Security Council, and the Bug Bounty Programme.
fact_verdicts.note: Stated in the anchor motivation.

fact_verdicts.fact: 7. The anchor states: "All expenditure will be fully accounted for and any unspent funds returned to the Cardano treasury."
fact_verdicts.note: Exact statement appears in the anchor motivation.

fact_verdicts.fact: 8. The anchor states execution of the work packages is contingent on approval of the IntersectMBO budget proposal.
fact_verdicts.note: Stated in the anchor note.

fact_verdicts.fact: 9. The anchor states the proposal achieved the required 67% support threshold during the 2026 Intersect Budget Process Hydra Voting phase and was advanced for on-chain Treasury Withdrawal Governance Action submission under the approved Budget Process Framework.
fact_verdicts.note: Stated in the Intersect Budget Process section.

fact_verdicts.fact: 10. The anchor states the requested amount does not, at time of submission, on its own or in aggregate, breach the applicable "350M Net Change Limit" covering Epoch 613 to Epoch 713, and does not exceed guardrail TREASURY-02a at the moment of submission.
fact_verdicts.note: The anchor states no breach of the applicable 350M NCL for Epoch 613 to Epoch 713 and, under TREASURY-02a, no NCL exceedance at submission.

fact_verdicts.fact: 11. The anchor states independent audits and assurance for this proposal will be provided by Appold, and reviews may include deliverable verification, control testing, spend validation, and assessment of milestone evidence where appropriate.
fact_verdicts.note: Stated in the Audit & Oversight section.

fact_verdicts.fact: 12. The anchor states Intersect will continue to operate milestone-based drawdowns, evidence-backed reporting, and transparent disclosures alongside the external assurance process.
fact_verdicts.note: Stated in the Audit & Oversight section.

fact_verdicts.fact: 13. The anchor states the Technical Steering Committee has not received funding from the Cardano Treasury within the past 24 months.
fact_verdicts.note: Stated in the Prior Treasury Funding Disclosure section.

fact_verdicts.fact: 14. The anchor discloses that Intersect and its committees have received Cardano Treasury funding within the past 24 months through three listed governance actions totaling "22,385,000" ada: 15,750,000 ada, 750,000 ada, and 5,885,000 ada.
fact_verdicts.note: The table lists those three amounts and total.

fact_verdicts.fact: 15. The anchor budget summary lists WP 1 — Community-Facing Technical Coordination: 256,000 ADA; WP 2 — Protocol Governance and Evolution: 832,000 ADA; WP 3 — Technical Review: 105,000 ADA; Total: 1,193,000 ADA.
fact_verdicts.note: Matches the Budget Summary table.

fact_verdicts.fact: 16. The anchor says Intersect will utilize the treasury management smart contract framework developed by Sundae Labs; a new 2026 instance mirrors the 2025 budget cycle contracts.
fact_verdicts.note: Stated in the Intersect Budget Management Tooling section.

fact_verdicts.fact: 17. The anchor lists the 2026 Treasury Reserve Smart Contract stake address as stake1784sdxt6jjennmstphgdu7l7c2scf5d02a6cve2dgn5s2kq5u3j9v, the 2026 Treasury Reserve Smart Contract payment address as addr1x84sdxt6jjennmstphgdu7l7c2scf5d02a6cve2dgn5s2k8tq6vh499n88hqkrwsmealas4psng674m4sej5638fq4vqmxs59w, and the 2026 Project Specific Smart Contract payment address as addr1x9d6k9z6t6fvsetj2djmerargk475lef9gfvshy4rwh4h7jm4v295h5jepjhy5m9hj86x3dtafljj2sjepwf2xa0t0aq048cay.
fact_verdicts.note: All three addresses match the anchor.

fact_verdicts.fact: 18. The anchor says Intersect will use a single Treasury Reserve Smart Contract with one Project-Specific Smart Contract; Intersect management consists of five admin and three leadership roles; an Oversight Committee of six external independent third-party entities provides checks and balances.
fact_verdicts.note: Stated in the Specifics section.

fact_verdicts.fact: 19. The anchor identifies the Oversight Committee entities as Sundae Labs, Cardano Foundation, Dquadrant, NMKR, Sundial, and Eternl.
fact_verdicts.note: The listed entities match the anchor.

fact_verdicts.fact: 20. The anchor specifies authorization thresholds for TRSC Fund and PSSC Modify, TRSC Disburse, TRSC Pause and Resume, TRSC Sweep, and TRSC Reorganize actions.
fact_verdicts.note: The anchor lists high-level permissions for each named action.

fact_verdicts.fact: 21. The anchor says upon enactment funding will be directed into the TRSC stake address; TRSC and PSSC instances cannot be staked with an SPO and are delegated to the auto-abstain predefined DRep.
fact_verdicts.note: Stated in the Processes section.

fact_verdicts.fact: 22. The anchor says when the legal contract is prepared and the vendor is ready, funding will be transferred using the Fund action to the PSSC, and all milestones will be outlined within the metadata.
fact_verdicts.note: Stated in the Processes section.

fact_verdicts.fact: 23. The anchor says a dashboard is available at treasury.sundae.fi for the community to audit the TRSC or PSSC and track metrics related to the withdrawn ada, with on-chain verifiability.
fact_verdicts.note: Stated in the Processes section with a treasury.sundae.fi dashboard link.

fact_verdicts.fact: 24. On-chain recipient history says stake address stake1784sdxt6jjennmstphgdu7l7c2scf5d02a6cve2dgn5s2kq5u3j9v appears in 14 withdrawal proposals totalling 57273260000000 lovelace, first 2026-06-23 16:14:33 and last 2026-06-26 13:51:52.
fact_verdicts.note: Matches Source Material 1 exactly.

fact_verdicts.fact: 25. On-chain DRep vote counts so far are yes=45, no=12, abstain=5, explicit ballots and not stake-weighted.
fact_verdicts.note: Matches Source Material 1 exactly.

fact_verdicts.fact: 26. Treasury flow over 36 epochs shows inflow 158838297854652 lovelace, enacted withdrawals 361435336000000 lovelace, ratio 2.275492.
fact_verdicts.note: Matches Source Material 1 exactly.

fact_verdicts.fact: 27. BEACN doctrine ranks treasury stewardship and downside protection second, after constitutional integrity and protocol safety, and ranks evidence quality and reproducibility third.
fact_verdicts.note: Matches the Values Hierarchy ordering.

fact_verdicts.fact: 28. BEACN doctrine rejects first-come-first-served budget models and adopts a rolling-window Net Change Limit framework that rewards proposal quality over submission timing.
fact_verdicts.note: Stated in the Treasury Spending Doctrine core position.

fact_verdicts.fact: 29. BEACN doctrine says if ecosystem growth conflicts with treasury stewardship and evidence is weak, prefer ABSTAIN; if treasury upside is plausible but non-verifiable, judge directionally on the available public record with a caution penalty, and in practice NO or ABSTAIN on thin asks.
fact_verdicts.note: Stated in the doctrine conflict handling defaults.

verification.summary: All 29 dossier facts are supported by the supplied source material. No contradicted facts or invented specific figures, names, dates, or commitments were found.
